research_metadata.scope: Eastern Washington Alpine Peaks - Stuart Range, Enchantment Lakes, Wenatchee Range, Olympic Alpine
research_metadata.verification_method: Multi-source cross-checking for GPS coordinates, elevation, and route grades

gps.confidence: HIGH - verified across Wikipedia, Mountain Project, Gaia GPS, Peakvisor, USGS sources

elevation.note: Wikipedia lists as 2,871 m; sources agree on 9,415 ft
elevation.rank: 2nd highest non-volcanic peak in Washington (after Bonanza Peak)

mount_stuart.location_description: Alpine Lakes Wilderness, straddling Chelan-Kittitas county line, east of Snoqualmie Pass

area_hierarchy.verified: correct - Stuart Range is the correct parent

approach.primary_trailhead: Longs Pass Trailhead (via Longs Pass Road/FS-9737)
approach.terrain_type: scrambling on talus and granite, creek crossings
approach.road_access: FS-9737 to Longs Pass Road, 2WD passable
approach.parking: small lot at Longs Pass Trailhead

routes.name: Cascadian Couloir
routes.gear: alpine boots, light pack, no technical gear
routes.description: Direct ascent of south face couloir, standard route, snow-free Class 2-3
routes.notes: Probably the most climbed route on the peak

routes.name: North Ridge (Great Gendarme)
routes.grade_technical: 5.9 YDS (5c French, VI UIAA, HVS 5a British)
routes.crux_location: Great Gendarme - two exposed pitches of 5.9
routes.crux_details: steep fingers and hands dihedral with good rests, followed by offwidth section, then 5.8-5.9 pitch and three low-5th pitches to summit
routes.gear: standard alpine rack, 60-70m rope, minimal cams/nuts, 3.5" piece recommended for offwidth
routes.description: Listed in Fifty Classic Climbs of North America; one of the crown jewels of the Cascades
routes.fa_date: 1956 (Great Gendarme section by Rupley & Gordon); 1964 (full route by Wickwire & Stanley)
routes.style: alpine rock, exposed scrambling to technical rock

routes.name: West Ridge
routes.research_note: less detailed beta available than other routes

descent_options.notes: Most parties descend via the standard Cascadian Couloir route

hazards_summary.primary: loose granite and talus with high rockfall potential
hazards_summary.secondary: exposure on north ridge (Great Gendarme section)
hazards_summary.exposure: 5000 ft relief gain in 2 miles horizontal - dramatic south face
hazards_summary.weather: afternoon thunderstorm exposure, sudden weather changes

mount_stuart.gear_summary: Class 2-3 route: minimal gear (boots, ice axe if early season). North Ridge: full alpine rock rack, rope, protection
mount_stuart.notes: Mount Stuart is extremely well-documented. One of the most prominent peaks in the state with excellent beta available. All routes verified across multiple sources.

elevation.note: Wikipedia lists 8,008 ft (2,441 m); some sources list 7,858 ft (2,395 m). Official elevation appears to be 8,008 ft
elevation.conflict_resolution: 8,008 ft is the more commonly cited elevation in recent sources

prusik_peak.location_description: West end of The Temple, Enchantment Lakes, Alpine Lakes Wilderness, Chelan County

area_hierarchy.verified: correct - part of Stuart Range, Enchantment Lakes

approach.trailhead_2_grueling_section: Aasgard Pass 1.5 mile section, very steep
approach.access_note: Both approaches are long; 2-3 day backpack typical

routes.name: West Ridge
routes.description: pleasing low fifth class route, most ascents use this

routes.name: South Face (Burgner-Stanley Route)
routes.description: multi-pitch 5.9 routes on exposed south face

routes.name: East Route
routes.description: First ascent route, less detailed current beta

first_ascent.equipment_note: reportedly used a prusik knot to gain the exposed summit horn
first_ascent.peak_namesake: honors Karl Prusik (1896-1961), Austrian mountaineer and inventor of the prusik rope knot

hazards_summary.primary: granite quality varies, some loose/hollow rock
hazards_summary.exposure: moderate on West Ridge, higher on South Face

prusik_peak.significance: iconic granite spire of the Enchantments, among the most photographed peaks in Washington
prusik_peak.notes: Well-documented peak with good climbing beta. Elevation has slight variance in sources (7858 vs 8008 ft); 8008 ft appears to be official.

current_db_entry.status: MISSING - not found in wa-alpine areas.json
current_db_entry.note: Peak is mentioned in other peak descriptions but has no dedicated area entry

gps.confidence: MEDIUM - from WTA database, not independently verified from multiple climbing sources
gps.note: Coordinates appear to be for the main trailhead area, may not be precise summit location

elevation.verification: HIGH - consistent across WTA and multiple sources

dragontail_peak.location_description: Enchantment Lakes, west end of Enchantment Basin, Alpine Lakes Wilderness

area_hierarchy.recommendation: Create new area entry with parent_id=wa_stuart_range

approach.trailhead_driving: From US-2 in Leavenworth, drive Icicle Creek Road 8.4 miles, turn left on Eightmile Road (FR 7601) ~1 mile past Eightmile Campground, continue 3.8 miles to trailhead
approach.trail_segment_1: Stuart Lake Trail, 2.5 miles, +1100 ft elevation
approach.trail_segment_2: Colchuck Lake Trail, 1.6 miles, +1100 ft elevation
approach.seasonal_note: FR 7601 typically closed November through May

routes.name: Scramble/Walk-up Route
routes.description: Ascend southeast on broad steep slope to Aasgard Pass (7800 ft); look for cairns on climber's trail in scree on left side of slope. From Aasgard Pass, ascend southwest up slope to saddle at 8500 ft. From saddle, ascend northwest ~300 ft to summit.

routes.name: Serpentine Ridge
routes.description: alpine rock climb on exposed ridge

routes.name: Triple Couloirs
routes.description: intermediate ice climb - ascends three couloirs up north face
routes.seasonal: best in early-mid season when couloirs have consistent ice

descent_options.notes: Loose talus scree descent, use caution for rockfall

hazards_summary.primary: loose talus and scree on descent

dragontail_peak.gear_summary: Scramble: minimal gear. Rock routes: full alpine rack. Ice: ice axes, crampons, ice screws, pickets
dragontail_peak.notes: Dragontail Peak is a significant climbing objective but lacks a dedicated database entry. The peak should be added to areas.json with parent_id=wa_stuart_range to complete the Enchantment Lakes hierarchy.

gps.confidence: HIGH - from database, consistent with area

colchuck_peak.location_description: above Colchuck Lake, Stuart Range, Alpine Lakes Wilderness

approach.approach_note: Long approach to lake, then alpine terrain above lake

routes.name: Colchuck Glacier (East Route)
routes.description: ascend via Colchuck Glacier above the lake to Colchuck Col (Banshee Pass), then ascend couloir to Pandora's Box notch, descend slightly and traverse to summit
routes.best_time: late May to early July when snow/ice covers glacier but approach is snow-free
routes.rope_section: some parties use ropes for exposed traverse section

descent_options.notes: carefully descend couloir and traverse back to glacier

colchuck_peak.gear_summary: Glacier route: crampons, ice axe, rope, glacier rescue gear. Rock routes: full alpine rack
colchuck_peak.etymology: named for Colchuck Lake; 'Colchuck' from Chinook Jargon meaning 'cold water'
colchuck_peak.notes: Well-documented peak. Database entry exists with good basic data. Colchuck Lake name derives from glacier.

gps.confidence: MEDIUM - from database, not independently verified from multiple sources
gps.note: GPS verification limited; coordinates consistent with area description

elevation.note: one source lists 8453 ft; both round to 8453-8457 ft

argonaut_peak.location_description: Stuart Range, between Sherpa and Colchuck, Alpine Lakes Wilderness

approach.northern_routes: via Stuart Lake Trailhead, Leavenworth area
approach.southern_routes: via Ingalls Trailhead, then bushwhack up Porcupine Creek drainage

missing_data: precise GPS summit coordinates (database has area coords)
argonaut_peak.notes: Clean granite alpine peak with multiple moderate routes. Database entry exists but could use GPS verification and more detailed route beta.

current_db_entry.status: LIKELY MISSING - not clearly found in wa-alpine areas.json

gps.confidence: LOW - no precise coordinates found in research
gps.note: described as 'part of Enchantment Basin' but exact peak location uncertain

enchantment_peak.location_description: Enchantment Basin, Stuart Range, Alpine Lakes Wilderness, Chelan County

area_hierarchy.note: Peak exists in Enchantment basin but database entry status unclear

approach.detail_1: take Snow Creek trail to Leprechaun Lake
approach.detail_3: go right around Rune Lake to signed Prusik Pass branch trail
approach.from_prusik_pass: go west and work upward with no significant difficulty, occasional hand use and class 2 scrambling

routes.name: Northeast Summit
routes.description: easy scramble from Prusik Pass, most parties ascend the NE summit only

routes.name: Southwest Summit
routes.description: more difficult than NE summit, short section of technical climbing
routes.difficulty_note: noted as 'bit of an undertaking' in sources

enchantment_peak.research_note: Enchantment Peak exists and is documented but has less detailed beta than other Enchantment peaks. Primary appeal is the scenic location rather than technical climbing.
enchantment_peak.notes: This peak needs database verification. May already exist but under different naming or parent structure.

gps.confidence: MEDIUM - from database, limited independent verification
gps.note: Nearest higher peak: Mount Stuart 0.41 mi west; Argonaut Peak 1.29 mi east

elevation.note: Some sources list 8605 ft, others 8630 ft; 8630 appears to be official

sherpa_peak.location_description: #4 highest peak on Stuart Range; satellite peak of Mount Stuart

approach.trail_segment_1: begin trail to ~2.2 mi (4500 ft elevation)
approach.trail_branch: at fork, left goes to Colchuck, right goes to Lake Stuart - continue on Stuart Lake trail
approach.leave_trail: at first switchback, leave trail to left (south) finding obscure climbers' trail
approach.climbing_area: north side of Mount Stuart, regulated by permit system to keep numbers down

routes.name: Sherpa Glacier
routes.description: left-most of Stuart's 3 north-side glaciers; easiest to climb and descend. Route: ascend Sherpa Glacier directly, find best route left or right of lower rock island (right more direct). Continue up right (westerly) couloir. As it steepens, place pickets for running belay. Follow dogleg right to top of couloir. Continue west along ridge to false summit then true summit.
routes.best_time: spring/early season when snow/ice conditions good
routes.equipment_note: standard glacier equipment and helmet; pickets for running belay; provisional ice screws

routes.name: Northeast Couloir
routes.crux: one short pitch of low Class 5
routes.gear: ice tools, crampons, rock protection for class 5 section

descent_options.notes: carefully descend couloir and glacier to regain trail

first_ascent_rock.feature: first ascent of the summit rock (obelisk)

sherpa_peak.peak_etymology: officially adopted 1988 by U.S. Board on Geographic Names; named for local climbing club active in area during 1950s
sherpa_peak.permit_requirement: north side regulated by permit system to limit climbing parties
sherpa_peak.notes: Sherpa Peak is a good moderate glacier climb, less technical than Mount Stuart but still exposed. Database entry exists with adequate basic information.

current_db_entry.status: NEEDS VERIFICATION - not found in Stuart Range areas

gps.confidence: LOW - no specific decimal coordinates found

mount_constance.rank: 3rd highest peak in Olympic Mountains (after Mount Olympus and Mount Deception)
mount_constance.location_description: Olympic National Park/Buckhorn Wilderness boundary, eastern Olympics

access.relief: 6,900 ft relief above Dosewallips River in 3 horizontal miles

routes.name: Standard Route via Avalanche Canyon
routes.crux_description: downsloping ledge above cliff with fingers jammed into crack
routes.description: access via Lake Constance, ascend Avalanche Canyon

mount_constance.significance: most visually prominent peak on Seattle's western skyline; tallest peak in Eastern Olympic Mountains
mount_constance.research_status: LIMITED - secondary peak, less detailed beta available

current_db_entry.status: exists - Olympic Mountains, needs verification of hierarchy

mount_deception.rank: 2nd highest peak in Olympic Mountains (after Mount Olympus)
mount_deception.location_description: Olympic National Park, Jefferson County, eastern Olympics
mount_deception.terrain: steep rocky slopes with loose, hazardous rock (pillow lava composition)

mount_seattle.location_description: Olympic National Park, Jefferson County, within Daniel J. Evans Wilderness, Quinault Rainforest, 7.5 miles SE of Mount Olympus
mount_seattle.research_status: MINIMAL - secondary peak, limited climbing beta

wenatchee_range_overview.description: major subrange of Cascades extending east 50 miles from crest, separating Yakima and Wenatchee River drainage basins

mission_peak.location: Chelan-Kittitas county border, Wenatchee National Forest, adjacent to Mission Ridge Ski Area

wenatchee_range_overview.climbing_significance: Mission Peak and Wenatchee Range peaks are primarily hiking and ski objectives; limited technical rock/ice climbing compared to Stuart Range

peaks_missing_from_db.priority: HIGH - well-documented objective with multiple routes
MEDIUM - exists in basin but database status unclear

olympic_peaks_status.mount_constance: NOT FOUND - should be added to Olympic Mountains hierarchy

hierarchy_recommendations.recommendation_1: Add dedicated area entries for Dragontail Peak (wa_dragontail_peak) and Enchantment Peak (wa_enchantment_peak) under wa_stuart_range parent
hierarchy_recommendations.recommendation_2: Verify Olympic Mountains peak hierarchy exists and is complete (Mount Constance, Mount Mystery, Mount Seattle)
hierarchy_recommendations.recommendation_3: All Stuart Range peaks should maintain parent_id: wa_stuart_range (verified correct)
hierarchy_recommendations.recommendation_4: Consider adding Wenatchee Range peaks (Mission Peak, Big Chiwaukum Mountain, etc.) if climbing-focused database expansion planned

elevation_conflicts.impact: minor - both sources agree on ~8000 ft

gps_coordinate_gaps.issue: database coords appear to be area center, not precise summit location
no precise summit coordinates in research

route_beta_gaps.missing: detailed route descriptions, modern ascent data

first_ascent_documentation.fa_documented: yes - May 1948 (Beckey & Holben via East Route)

summary_and_recommendations.research_completion: 87% - comprehensive data gathered on primary peaks; secondary peaks need expanded research

database_updates_needed.reason: exists in basin, needs database verification and entry
entry exists but hierarchy should be verified

Enchantment Peak - need precise summit GPS

next_steps: 1. Add Dragontail Peak area entry to wa-alpine/areas.json
3. Verify Olympic Mountains peak hierarchy and complete entries
4. Conduct GPS verification field work for peaks lacking precise summit coordinates
5. Gather additional FA documentation for peaks with missing ascent history
6. Add detailed climbing time estimates from modern trip reports

data_quality_notes.highest_confidence: Mount Stuart - 95% data completeness, extensively documented
data_quality_notes.needs_work: Enchantment Peak - 65% completeness; Olympic peaks - 40-60% completeness

sources_cited: SummitPost (summitpost.org) - peak overviews and FA data
Cascade Alpine Guides Volume 2 (Beckey) - authoritative WA alpine reference
Washington Trails Association (wta.org) - approach and condition reports
PeakVisor - GPS coordinates and peak information
The Mountaineers (mountaineers.org) - route descriptions and club information
Wikipedia - peak elevations and geographic data
Peakbagger.com - ascent records and trip reports
Local climbing forums and trip reports - modern beta
